projects.title: Airlift Capability Project - Multi-role Flight Service
projects.objective: ACP-MFS will provide the Royal Canadian Air Force (RCAF) the ability to perform worldwide utility flights such as aeromedical evacuations, humanitarian disaster assistance response team transport, deployments in support of national security, and administrative flight services at a reduced environmental footprint.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Ground Based Air Defence
projects.objective: The GBAD system will provide tactical air defence protection to friendly forces and vital installations during expeditionary and domestic operations against the increasingly diverse air threat.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Loitering Munitions UOR
projects.objective: The CAF has an urgent operational requirement for a Loitering Munition weapon system to provide CA forces on Op REASSURANCE with an organic precision-fires weapon system able to neutralize Russian Federation (RUS) combat support capabilities beyond visual line of sight to a range of 40 km.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Advanced Improvised Explosive Device (IED) Detection and Defeat
projects.objective: The objective of the project is to enhance the Canadian Armed Forces capability to mitigate the threat of improvised explosive devices on future operations. It will improve the Canadian Armed Forces’ ability to anticipate, analyse and protect against improvised explosive device attacks.

kics.label: Remotely Piloted Systems and Autonomous Technologies

projects.title: Night Vision System Modernization
projects.objective: This project will improve the soldier's ability to operate in reduced visibility conditions with lower operator payload leveraging next generation technology.

projects.title: Light Forces Enhancement
projects.objective: LFE will deliver a mobile, light weight, all-terrain vehicle to enhance the mobility of the Canadian Army's Light Forces. This will enable them to function with increased range and response time while reducing overall soldier burden during operations within complex terrain and unique operations environments.

projects.title: Advanced Water Supply System
projects.objective: This project is to replace and improve the Canadian Armed Forces water supply and distribution capability during operations. The project will procure an integrated system of water production, storage, and distribution. A new light water production capability will also be introduced at the tactical level (company level or below).

projects.title: Camp Sustain
projects.objective: This project will deliver new camp utilities to improve energy efficiencies and reduce the environmental footprint of military base/compounds in an operational environment. CS project will deliver a deployable camp infrastructure and utilities system to provide sustained, robust and flexible support during CAF expeditionary and domestic operations. CS will sustain up to four concurrent deployed task forces of 250 to 1500 personnel for concurrent operations, or up to 4800 pers for a single operation for deployment duration of six to more than 24 months.

projects.title: Joint Deployable HQ and Signal Regiment Modernization
projects.objective: The project will improve 1 Canadian Division Headquarters’ ability to fulfill the joint deployable headquarters function and the Joint Signal Regiment’s ability to provide integral support to the joint headquarters, as well as a national rear link function to all significant international and domestic operations and other assigned national tasks.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Tactical Power System
projects.objective: To replace many CAF generators under 70 kW. For some systems, this will include new distribution systems, power management, and energy storage to achieve higher fuel savings.

projects.title: Tactical Power System Sustainment Contract
projects.objective: The Project Management Office will be establishing one long-term contract to sustain the TPS eqpt fleet IAW the approved sustainment strategy from the DPS2 Governance meeting of 13 Jan 2023.

projects.title: aDEFENCEX
projects.objective: aDefenceX project is undertaking an ERP transformation to modernize operational processes, optimize resource allocation, and enhance readiness in a rapidly evolving security environment. By undertaking this transformation, DND can improve decision-making capabilities, streamline logistics, and enhance mission effectiveness, ultimately ensuring the agility and effectiveness required to meet evolving national defence needs while maximizing resource efficiency.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Advanced Sub-Unit Water Purification System
projects.objective: The project will procure an integrated system of water purification, storage, distribution, transportation and sustainment equipment to support the supply of water to Canadian Armed Forces sub-units in training and on operations. In order to realize procurement efficiencies, this project will incorporate the water production and distribution requirements of the Royal Canadian Air Force (RCAF) and Canadian Joint Operations Command (CJOC).

projects.title: Automatic Identification Technology
projects.objective: The AIT Project will acquire and integrate readily available Commercial Off-the-Shelf (COTS) marking/reading technologies into Defence Resource Management Information System (DRMIS) to automate supply chain business processes and data capture and entry capabilities at designated DND/CAF supply unit locations including those deployed in Canada and abroad. The capability will enhance asset visibility and, in turn, facilitate increased operational readiness, material and fuel management. The project contributes to Defence Policy (SSE) initiatives 29-58, 60, 62, 63, 64, 67 and 105.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Bridge and Gap Crossing Modernization
projects.objective: All military vehicles have some inherent off-road mobility over rough terrain; however, they require assistance to overcome natural and man-made obstacles such as soft soil, destroyed bridges, rivers, canals, ditches, urban rubble, road craters and minefields. The Bridge and Gap Crossing Modernization project will procure a family of new bridging systems to replace aging equipment and address gap crossing capability deficiencies.

projects.title: Common Heavy Equipment Replacement
projects.objective: This project will improve Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) mobility, counter-mobility, force protection and sustainment capabilities by recapitalizing existing fleets of Heavy Support Equipment (HSE). It will be capable of activating and operating extended lines of communication from Canadian ports of embarkation to theatre ports of disembarkation. It will include capabilities to conduct large scale horizontal construction activities ranging from runway repairs to road construction and maintenance.

kics.label: Remotely Piloted Systems and Autonomous Technologies

projects.title: International Household Goods and Effects Relocation Services
projects.objective: Services to manage and execute, on an as and when requested basis, the physical movement of the personal Household Goods and Effects (HG&E) and PMVs of relocating federal government employees (i.e. the Shipper) who are transferred between Canada and international locations.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: LAV Specialist Variant Enhancements (LAV SVE)
projects.objective: This project will group three previously separate projects into a single project relating to the LAV III Upgrade specialist vehicle variants including:

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Meridian Standard
projects.objective: Meridian Standard will be a globally deployable and highly mobile land asset with strategic, operation and tactical Signals Intelligence and Cyber capabilities that supports CAF and CFIOG operations.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: National Stocktaking Program(Renewal)
projects.objective: The Department of National Defence (DND) requires the services of a contractor to assist in the physical stocktaking, data validation and correction, directed independent asset inventory verification and other processes and procedures for inventory management of Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) warehouses and contractor facilities holding government owned inventory across Canada in support of a robust Stocktaking and Inventory Management Program (SIMP).

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Close Support Bridging System
projects.objective: This project will deliver a common family of bridges optimized for short support (up to 14 m) and medium support (up to 26 m) that can be employed in assault and tactical roles to support mechanized forces. The Assault Bridge Launcher (ABL) chassis will be based on the Leopard 2 and the Tactical Bridge Launcher (TBL) will be based on a wheeled platform in-service with Canada or NATO allies. Both chassis are currently in service with the NATO and within the CA this would ensure commonality of the vehicle fleet but more importantly a proven track record in meeting the operational conditions. This will reduce the need for reliability, availability, maintainability, and durability (RAMD) testing on respective chassis.

kics.label: Remotely Piloted Systems and Autonomous Technologies

projects.title: Enhanced Recovery Capability
projects.objective: This project will replace the Canadian Armed Forces Heavy Logistic Vehicle Wheeled (HLVW) Wrecker/Recovery configuration. It will transform the recovery portion of the sustain capability by enabling modern technologies to effectively recover the newer and heavier armoured fighting and logistics vehicles that cannot currently be recovered in a safe and suitable manner.

kics.label: Remotely Piloted Systems and Autonomous Technologies

projects.title: Future Combined Aerospace Operations Center Capability
projects.objective: To improve the survivability, infrastructure, and the system integration at the Combined Aerospace Operations Centre.

projects.title: Domestic Arctic Mobility Enhancement
projects.objective: This project will replace the current BV206 fleet with a new arctic mobility capability consisting of a fleet of medium-sized, amphibious, high mobility carriers.

projects.title: Light Utility Vehicle
projects.objective: To replace the in-service Light Utility Vehicle Wheeled fleets.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: CH-149 Cormorant Mid-Life Upgrade
projects.objective: This project will extend the estimated life expectancy of the CH149 Cormorant to at least 2042, address obsolescence issues, ensure compliance with emerging regulations, and address a search and rescue capability deficiency at the Trenton Main Operating Base. These objectives will be achieved by way of updates and upgrades to the existing CH149 Cormorant fleet, augmentation of the fleet, and improvements to maintenance regimes and training.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: River-Class Destroyer (RCD)
projects.objective: To recapitalize the Royal Canadian Navy's surface combatant fleet by replacing and updating the capabilities found in both the retired Iroquois-class destroyers and the multi-role Halifax-class frigates and provide the necessary ammunition, training and support. The new River-class Destroyer (RCD) will ensure that Canada can continue to monitor and defend its waters and make significant contributions to international naval operations.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

dcias.label: Marine Sub-Systems and Platform Management Systems

kics.label: Marine Ship-Borne Mission and Platform Systems
Remotely Piloted Systems and Autonomous Technologies

projects.title: Joint Support Ship
projects.objective: To deliver two Joint Support Ships to replace the Royal Canadian Navy’s Auxiliary Oiler Replenishment vessels that have reached the end of their service. The capabilities required of the Joint Support Ships are crucial to the Royal Canadian Navy. These new Protecteur-class ships will enable a Naval Task Group to remain at sea for extended periods of time. These vessels will provide core replenishment capabilities, plus added capacity for limited sealift and limited support to operations ashore.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

dcias.label: Marine Sub-Systems and Platform Management Systems

kics.label: Marine Ship-Borne Mission and Platform Systems
Remotely Piloted Systems and Autonomous Technologies

projects.title: Remote Minehunting and Disposal System
projects.objective: To develop a modular, stand-off Naval Mine Countermeasures capability, designed to provide the full spectrum of Naval Mine Hunting operations and contribute to underwater domain awareness. The Remote Minehunting and Disposal Systems (RMDS) will generate the capability to detect, classify, localize, reacquire, identify, and dispose of sea mines and Underwater Improvised Explosive Devices that pose a threat to Canadian interests or impede the conduct of maritime operations. The RMDS project intends to leverage proven Commercial off the Shelf unmanned systems and Autonomous Underwater Vehicle (AUV) technology. These RMDS payloads will contain modular sub-systems that are portable and deployable.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

kics.label: Remotely Piloted Systems and Autonomous Technologies

projects.title: Brigade Communications Information System UOR
projects.objective: The objective of the Brigade Communications Information Systems (CIS) Urgent Operational Requirement (UOR) is to deliver various communications equipment and supporting software packages to address the operational disadvantages that currently exist in the Canadian Army’s (CA) tactical network for its Operation REASSURANCE mission.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Electronic Health Record Platform
projects.objective: Deliver an Electronic Health Record (EHR) system providing modern clinical decision-support tools, advanced analytics, and a patient experience commensurate with our provincial and defence partners.

projects.title: Information Technology Infrastructure in Support of Command and Control
projects.objective: The Information Technology Infrastructure (ITI) in Support of Command and Control (ITI in Sp of C2) project will provide secure, disruption tolerant, information exchange capability, employing technology that is interoperable with our Joint, Interagency, Multinational and Public partners, which can be readily evolved to meet future challenges. The project will replace the existing Consolidated Secret Network Infrastructure (CSNI), and the numerous single purpose classified networks across the Department of National Defence and Canadian Armed Forces (DND/CAF), increasing the ability to use, maintain, protect, upgrade and defend the ITI, and bolstering effective command, control, communications, computers, intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance (C4ISR) at all levels, spanning across security classifications and caveats. This investment contributes to the realization of the Military Integrated Information Infrastructure (MI3) described in the Canadian Armed Forces C4ISR Strategic Vision, Goals and Objectives.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Land Command Support System Tactical Communications Modernization
projects.objective: The project will improve the ability of tactical commanders and their staffs to exchange planning and execution information by modernising and re-capitalising the in-service tactical communication systems. The project will provide robust, reliable, and adaptive bearer systems and management tools for use in connecting mission elements engaged in land operations.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Next Generation Military Pay System
projects.objective: Implementation of a modern, scalable compensation and benefits delivery capability, integrated within the overall CAF policy, administrative and personnel management framework.

projects.title: Air Defence System UOR
projects.objective: AD-UOR will acquire a Very Short Range Air Defence (VSHORAD) System for OP Reassurance in Latvia in order to counter the assessed air threat within the Baltic region.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Land Anti-X System
projects.objective: The Land Anti-X Project will provide a modern family of anti-tank and anti-structure capabilities in both dismounted and mounted vehicle configurations.

projects.title: Portable Anti-X Missile UOR
projects.objective: The current TOW ITAS is obsolete and ineffective to be deployed into Operation REASSURANCE in Latvia. A modern soldier-portable missile system capable of defeating any Main Battle Tank (MBT) faced in operations was deemed essential for NATO credibility and mission success.

projects.title: Victoria-class Modernization (VCM)
projects.objective: The Victoria-class Modernization (VCM) Project will provide modernized and increased capability which will maintain the Victoria-class submarines’ (VCS) operational relevance through the mid-2030s. VCM is a series of 13 individual projects implemented on a common platform to generate the necessary capability for Canada’s submarines. Overall, the modernization outcomes will make Canada and its allies stronger and safer by:

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

dcias.label: Marine Sub-Systems and Platform Management Systems

kics.label: Marine Ship-Borne Mission and Platform Systems
Remotely Piloted Systems and Autonomous Technologies

projects.title: Cyber Defence - Decision Analysis and Response
projects.objective: The Cyber Defence – Decision Analysis and Response (CD-DAR) Project will acquire defensive cyber capabilities to improve overall decision support and security of the DND/CAF networks, including the ability to detect, analyze and respond to threats. The integrated solution will provide reliable contextual analysis to support DND/CAF decisions and actions within designated Command Network (Comd-Net); Extensions and Interfaces, and deployable Defence Wide Area Network (DWAN) systems to support the conduct of Defensive Cyber Operations (DCO).

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Defence CMP Secure Radio Sub-Project
projects.objective: To replace and modernize selected legacy secure radio equipment within the Department of National Defence that is approaching cryptographic End of Life and replace those existing legacy capabilities in inventory for which modern replacements are most urgently required.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: DEFENCEx
projects.objective: DRBM Programme (inclusive of the DEFENCEx Project) -critical to DND’s efforts to digitize and modernize its Defence resource management business

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Enhanced Satellite Communications Project - Polar
projects.objective: The Enhanced Satellite Communications Project – Polar (ESCP-P) will provide the Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) with Ultra-High Frequency (UHF) narrowband and wideband satellite communications (SATCOM), essential for Beyond Line Of Sight communications in the Arctic. The project was initially part of the Tactical Narrowband SATCOM (TNS) but the Arctic SATCOM capability for the Canadian North and Joint Task Force North Area of Responsibility was moved to this project.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Identity Credential Access Management
projects.objective: The Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) Identity, Credential and Access Management Project will support a unique identity for each person and network device accessing CAF information resources. A standardized and centrally managed identification and credentialing process will be instituted.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Land Intelligence Surveillance Reconnaissance Modernization
projects.objective: This project will dramatically improve the Canadian Army ability to force generate land-based SENSE capability by standardizing and normalizing existing battlefield systems dissemination and control mechanisms and introducing select state-of-the art sensors to the field army where gaps exists.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Network Command Control Integrated Situation Awareness Capability
projects.objective: The Network Command and Control Integrated Situational Awareness Capability will provide the situational awareness to the commanders of those Informational Technology (IT) services which are critical to the success of their operations.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Royal Canadian Navy Intelligence, Surveillance, Target Acquisition and Reconnaissance Unmanned Aircraft System
projects.objective: The RCN ISTAR UAS project will provide an alternate airborne platform that can be operated from the Halifax-class frigate in order to provide near real-time, Intelligence, Surveillance, Target Acquisition, and Reconnaissance information. This ability will provide critical over the horizon Situational Awareness and generate an operational advantage for commanders while minimizing the risk to the frigate or Maritime Helicopter in support of simple to multi-threat operations.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

kics.label: Remotely Piloted Systems and Autonomous Technologies

projects.title: Tactical Narrowband Satellite Communications-Geosynchronous
projects.objective: The Tactical Narrowband Satellite Communications – Geosynchronous (TNS-GEO) project will deliver a Beyond Line of Sight Ultra-High Frequency (UHF) narrowband satellite communications (SATCOM) capability to the Department of National Defence (DND) and the Canadian Armed Forces (CAF). TNS-GEO will leverage the existing Mobile User Objective System (MUOS), a military SATCOM system owned and operated by the United States (U.S.) Department of Defence (DoD), to fulfil DND/CAF’s tactical narrowband SATCOM capability requirements. Arctic communications above 65° N will be provided through the Enhanced Satellite Communications Project – Polar (ESCP-P).

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Canadian Multi-Mission Aircraft
projects.objective: To equip the Canadian Armed Forces with a long-range, long-endurance, multi-mission capability, optimized in Command, Control, Communications and Computers, Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (C4ISR), Anti-Submarine Warfare (ASW) and Anti-Surface Warfare (ASuW) to replace the CP140.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Containerized On-Board Reelable Array
projects.objective: The COBRA project will deliver a transportable, underwater detection and tracking capability for the AOPVs to enhance their current mission sets. Increasing the CAF’s capabilities in our Arctic and offshore waters is one of the key requirements of the AOPVs and the addition of an easily transportable underwater sensor suite will help the ship contribute to the defence of Canada and North America. Specifically, while the class is not considered a combatant, having a sensor suite to contribute to the shared awareness and understanding of undersea activities occurring in Canadian maritime approaches, maritime areas, and internal waterways is an important contribution to NORAD’s maritime warning mission.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

dcias.label: Marine Sub-Systems and Platform Management Systems

projects.title: Defence Enhanced Surveillance from Space - Project (DESSP)
projects.objective: DESSP will replace and improve upon the defence capabilities delivered by the GC RADARSAT Constellation Mission (RCM) and Polar Epsilon 1 and 2 projects.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Halifax-class Combat Systems Integration In-Service Support Contract
projects.objective: These services are in support of the Combat Management System 330 (CMS 330) for the Halifax-class ships and associated shore-based engineering, training, testing and range facilities used to support the HCS combat systems until class End of Life (EoL). This includes the sustainment and capability growth of the CMS 330.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Joint Fires Modernization
projects.objective: Joint Fires Modernization Project (JFM) will acquire modern hardware and software that will enable the Canadian Army (CA) to digitally share fire support and targeting information across the tactical and operational levels, into the CAF Joint Targeting Enterprise, and with partners and allies. Additionally, it will enable Canada to meet international interoperability commitments with the United States, the Five Eyes (FVEY) Alliance, and the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO), manage obsolescence issues, and achieve enhanced operator readiness through resource-efficient training.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence
Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Underwater Warfare Suite Upgrade (UWSU)
projects.objective: To modernize the underwater warfare sensor suite that is currently installed on the Halifax Class frigates. The Underwater Warfare Suite Upgrade (UWSU) project will improve the performance of the underwater sensors through the upgrade and/or replacement of the components of the underwater warfare sensor suite.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

dcias.label: Marine Sub-Systems and Platform Management Systems

projects.title: Manned Airborne ISR (MAISR)
projects.objective: The project will provide an operational level, multi-sensor MAISR capability to support both Special Operations Forces and Canadian Armed Forces domestic and international operations.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Protected Military Satellite Communication
projects.objective: The PMSC project is providing the CF with a protected, Worldwide MILSATCOM capability. The project includes MILSATCOM terminals for ships and submarines, strategic communications units, and deployed land, sea and air forces.The PMSC project is providing the CF with a protected, Worldwide MILSATCOM capability. The project includes MILSATCOM terminals for ships and submarines, strategic communications units, and deployed land, sea and air forces.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: CH146 Griffon Limited Life Extension Project
projects.objective: The project will extend the life of the CH-146 Griffon beyond the current Estimated Life Expectancy so that the aircraft can continue to be operationally relevant and remain a vital contributor to the readiness of the Canadian Army, the Canadian Special Operations Force Command, as well as the Search and Rescue posture, well into the future. The extension will bridge the gap until a replacement capability is acquired through the Next Tactical Aviation Capability Set project.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

kics.label: Remotely Piloted Systems and Autonomous Technologies

projects.title: Multi-Fleet Air Traffic Management Avionics
projects.objective: To upgrade Communications Navigation Surveillance Air Traffic Management (CNS/ATM) avionics systems in a number of Royal Canadian Air Force (RCAF) fleets to allow operations in future domestic and international airspace.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: RCAF Air Weapons Control Coverage Expansion
projects.objective: The RCAF Air Weapons Control Coverage expansion (RACE) project will replace the technically obsolescent collection of 189 paired DND/CAF owned UHF radios installed at 81 NAVCAN transmission sites located across Canada, and install new equipment at other key locations in Canada’s North to expand radio coverage with a networked digital UHF radio capability for continued use by both NAVCAN and DND/CAF.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Secure Radio Modernization Project
projects.objective: To deliver secure radios to CAF air and special forces.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Strategic Radio Capability
projects.objective: SRC will modernize the High Frequency (HF) communications to ensure that a sovereign, terrestrial-based C2 capability exists to support domestic (including Arctic) and expeditionary communications for CAF elements. It is a complementary and alternative to SATCOM for the provision of global C2.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Voice Switch for Air Traffic Control Units
projects.objective: The project will modernize legacy voice communication switches and voice recorder equipment that manage, integrate and record all voice communications within the Air Traffic Control (ATC) units and Military Flight Advisory units at Royal Canadian Air Force (RCAF) Wings.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Naval Communications Modernization (NCM)
projects.objective: The NCM Project will modernize existing RCN radio communications capabilities for the Halifax Class Ships (HCS) and the Victoria Class Ships (VCS) with modern, software-defined, software-reprogrammable and software-upgradable secure voice, data and LINK-16 radio communications capabilities (HCS only), to sustain interoperability with the remainder of the CAF, US, FVEY2 partners and North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO).

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Secure Command and Control Mobile Device - Secured Tailored Solutions
projects.objective: Project is to provide user access to classified electronic information and to perform command and control functions outside of DND/CAF security zones.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Instrument Landing System Replacement
projects.objective: To examine the precision appraoch requirements at Royal Canadian Airfields with the aim of optimizing systems for the future operating environment.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Utility Transport Aircraft
projects.objective: The Royal Canadian Air Force (RCAF) must support the Canadian Armed Forces’ (CAF) northern presence with an effective, relevant and enhanced utility airlift of resource to remote locations.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Canadian Cryptographic Modernization Program (CCMP) Classified Security Management Infrastructure (CSMI)
projects.objective: To implement an automated, network accessible, electronic-based key management and cryptographic product delivery system that will support the secure generation, production, distribution, management, and auditing of cryptographic products.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Defence Cryptographic Modernization Project (DCPM) - Omnibus
projects.objective: The Defence Cryptographic Modernization Project is an Omnibus project that will replace affected Type 1 End Cryptographic Units in the DND/CAF inventory. This project was initiated as a result of a similar modernization program within the US.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: CF Land Electronic Warfare Modernization
projects.objective: The Canadian Armed Forces must be able to employ the electromagnetic spectrum effectively as an integral aspect of all military operations, while selectively denying or exploiting the adversary's use of it and protecting Canadian Armed Forces from the effects of hostile action taken using electromagnetic measures. The Canadian Forces Land Electronic Warfare Modernization project will improve the Canadian Armed Forces’ ability to monitor, manage and deliberately deny the use of the electromagnetic spectrum.

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

kics.label: Remotely Piloted Systems and Autonomous Technologies

projects.title: CC130J Block Upgrade
projects.objective: To update the baseline C130J fleet to ensure the fleet is compliant with military and civilian regulations, and will maintain the aircraft’s ability to operate alongside allied forces through the incorporation of technological requirements

dcas.label: Command, Control, Communications, Computers and Intelligence

projects.title: Surveillance of Space 2
projects.objective: The aim of the Surveillance of Space 2 project (SofS 2) is to address national Space Situational Awareness (SSA) requirements, strategic relevance and allied burden sharing. SofS 2 will enable the RCAF to continue to perform deep space surveillance for the protection of Canadian deep space assets, mission assurance for space-based capabilities that support the CAF, and to ensure that Canada is an active contributor to the operations of the Space Surveillance Network (SSN).

projects.title: Canadian Forces Electronic Warfare Support
projects.objective: To deliver an enterprise-level Electronic Warfare Operational Support System (EWOSS) and reprogramming testing and validation capability.

projects.title: River-class Destroyer In-Service Support Contract
projects.objective: The Project Management Office for the River-class Destroyer (RCD) may establish one or more In-Service Support (ISS) contracts based upon the overall RCD Support Solution that will be defined.

dcias.label: Marine Sub-Systems and Platform Management Systems

kics.label: Marine Ship-Borne Mission and Platform Systems

projects.title: Airborne Early Warning & Control
projects.objective: This project will deliver a Canadian AEWC capability in order to further contribute to the NORAD Aerospace Warning and Control (AWAC) missions, achieve greater strategic effects in a rapidly evolving security environment, and reinforce Canada’s support to peace and stabilization operations.

projects.title: Canadian Advanced Synthetic Environment
projects.objective: The aim of this project is to establish network-based infrastructure and processes for Distributed Mission Training, Joint Mission Rehearsal, and air & ground crew training.

projects.title: Land Vehicle Crew Training System
projects.objective: The Land Vehicle Crew Training System project will deliver a training system, connected by a network and housed in purpose built facilities, to enable crews of the principle armoured combat vehicles to train as realistically as possible at their home garrison locations.

projects.title: Recap Gagetown Range and Training Area
projects.objective: Upgrade roads in a training area to meet the needs of the modern Canadian Armed Forces while improving safety and environmental compliance. Infrastructure consolidation and range improvements will enhance training capabilities, with a focus on long-term sustainability.

projects.title: Weapon Effects Simulation Modernization
projects.objective: The Project will upgrade or replace the current hardware and software used by the Weapons Effects Simulation (WES) systems at Canadian Army bases. The Project will augment instrumentation and simulation equipment to increase the realism of joint collective training exercises. The Project will take advantage of technological advances to increase the effectiveness and efficiency of training and the After Action Review process.

projects.title: Operational Training Infrastructure Enterprise Modernization
projects.objective: Modernization of RCAF training environments to operationalize F-35 and other future RCAF capabilities.

projects.title: Construct Cambrai Rd Training Quarters - Borden
projects.objective: Construction of a new training quarters building of 18,200 m2.

projects.title: Junior Non-Commission Member Training Accommodation Facility, CFB Esquimalt
projects.objective: New Facility to provide CFB Esquimalt a centralized, modern and technologically current accommodations capability that aligns with departmental directives for both accommodation and environmental standards.

projects.title: Canadian Special Operations Training Centre Infrastructure (CSOTC) - Petawawa
projects.objective: Construct permanent infrastructure to CSOTC.

projects.title: Joint Task Force 2 Infrastructure
projects.objective: Project includes the construction of purpose-built facility.

projects.title: National Defence Secure Campus
projects.objective: Provision of facilities to accommodate services in support to CF Command / Control & Intelligence.

projects.title: Halifax Heating and Municipal Services Upgrades
projects.objective: The project will provide a safe, sustainable, economical, and effective source of heat and steam for Dockyard and Stadacona, along with municipal services upgrades.

projects.title: Recapitalize Roads And Utilities - 19 Wing Comox
projects.objective: Recapitalize the roads and utilities of 19 Wing Comox.

projects.title: 427 SOAS Hangar Replacement, Petawawa
projects.objective: This project will replace the original hangar constructed in 1973 and extended in the mid 90s to accommodate additional aircraft. It will address health and safety issues which plague the facility leaving workers and aircraft at significant risk. Insufficient work and storage facilities places the unit operational mission in jeopardy. The project will deliver a modern operational hangar with a view to integrating command, control, communications, coordination, intelligence and aviation asset readiness enabling the force generation of high readiness special operations aviation elements in support of SOTFs for domestic and international operations. Project design will take into consideration the replacement of the Griffon helicopter under the nTACS Project.

projects.title: A/B Jetty Recapitalization - Esquimalt
projects.objective: Replace the existing major operational jetties (A and B)

projects.title: Accommodate 436 Transport Squadron - 8 Wing Trenton
projects.objective: The acquisition and delivery of new CC-130J-30 Hercules aircraft has created a need for modernized maintenance facilities. 436 Transport Squadron requires a facility to enable its role in supporting the CAF mandate of force mobility.

projects.title: 21 Electronic Warfare Regiment - CFB Kingston
projects.objective: To provide adequate facility to the 2 Electronic Warfare Squadron.

projects.title: Consolidate Fleet Diving Unit (Pacific)
projects.objective: The aim of the FDU(P) Consolidation project is to replace the majority of the existing 43 FDU(P) terrestrial facilities and the entire marine infrastructure with three modern buildings and a new floating jetty. This will provide modern, dafe, functionally-suitable and sustainable facilities to support the current and future operations of FDU(P).

projects.title: Construct 2 Service Battalion Headquarters and Administration Company Facility - CFB Petawawa
projects.objective: To resolve critical infrastructure requirements for the Administration Company of 2 Service Battalion.

projects.title: Construct CAF TC, 3 CDSB Edmonton, AB
projects.objective: This project will deliver new infrastructure to accommodate the CAF Transition Group (CAF TG) organization, including the Transition Center for 3 CDSB Edmonton and CAF TU AB-North in one centralized location.

projects.title: Edmonton Strategy
projects.objective: This project is intended to identify and implement a solution for optimization and fit-up of office furniture and IT support systems based on the functional space analysis which was conducted with the client/users following GC workplace standards and DND hybrid working directives. This optimization will focus on user security requirements, new GC workplace fit-up standards, and providing a flexible environment.

projects.title: Construct New Communications and Information Systems Building - Petawawa
projects.objective: Replace the existing small building and the attached pre-fab structure to accommodate communication and information systems in an efficient infrastructure conditions.

projects.title: AFEC Main Facility, CFB Bagotville
projects.objective: To construct a new purpose built facility to accommodate the admin and operation requirements for 2 Wing the RCAF's Air Expeditionary Support Unit.

projects.title: AFEC Mess, Dining and Accomodations, CFB Bagotville
projects.objective: To construct a new building to house tree new Mess, Kitchen & Dining, and Accommodation for the RCAF's 2 and 3 Wing.

projects.title: Canadian Joint Incident Response Unit / Infrastructure - Trenton
projects.objective: Provision of Full Operational Capability infrastructure to accommodate the growth of this unit.

projects.title: Consolidate Military Family Resource Centre (MFRC) North Town Site - CFB Petawawa
projects.objective: Provide a purpose built facility for the CFB Petawawa MFRC.

projects.title: Construct Ammunition Workshop - DRDC Valcartier
projects.objective: The project will replace the ammunition workshop building.

projects.title: Construct Health Services Centre - CFB Bagotville
projects.objective: This project will provide to Canadian Forces Health Services (CFHS) Bagotville with the operational capability they need to carry out their mission to provide services to prepare, protect, and sustain force health to ensure readiness for and successful achievement of core CAF. The aim of the project is to provide adequate accommodation for CFHS Bagotville with a new Health Care Centre (HCC) with a gross surface area of 5,993 m2 and demolish the main building (B66) and the Tank storage building (B160) (2.050m +2m) totalling 2052 m2.

projects.title: Construct Laboratory Facilities - DRDC ORC
projects.objective: This project will deliver modern, purpose-built infrastructure to provide Defence Research and Development Canada (DRDC) with reliable, secure facilities at Shirley's Bay Campus with a view to integrating DRDC’s science and technology teams with essential operational support resources, as well as providing layered physical security to successfully deliver the Ottawa Research Centre’s (ORC’s) mandate. It will contribute to the federal government mandate to rebuild Canada for the 21st Century through green building strategies to improve portfolio sustainability.

projects.title: Construct Multi-purpose Building (MPB) - Yellowknife
projects.objective: Acquisition or construction of a new Multi-purpose building in Yellowknife.

projects.title: Energetic Materials Property Test Lab - DRDC Valcartier
projects.objective: This project will build a laboratory of approximately 300 m2 to house the facilities for assessing the properties of energy materials, at DRDC.

projects.title: Greenwood Runway 12-30 Recapitalization
projects.objective: This project will rebuild runway 12/30 to address the secondary runway's infrastructure deficiencies.

projects.title: Health Services Centre - Gagetown
projects.objective: Construct a new annex attached to the existing clinic and renovate the existing clinic to accommodate both medical and dental under one facility.

projects.title: Health Services Centre - Valcartier
projects.objective: Upgrade the health services centre in Valcartier.

projects.title: Infrastructure Renewal of DRDC - Valcartier
projects.objective: Upgrade the existing facility of Defence Research & Development of Canada.

projects.title: Nanisivik Naval Facility -Targeted Structural Repairs
projects.objective: To repair the corrosion in the jetties at Nanisivik to prevent failure.

projects.title: Neurobehavioral Facility for Blast Injury Assessment - Suffield
projects.objective: Provide upgrades to the existing 1.8m Blast Tube at the Ritzel Centre.

projects.title: New Quick Reaction Area - Bagotville
projects.objective: Construction of a new Quick Reaction Area to accommodate the operations of the fighter Aircrafts in operation at 3 Wing Bagotville.

projects.title: Rehabilitate and Fit-up North Park Armoury - Halifax
projects.objective: The two-phase project will ensure the historic, 120-year-old armoury continues to be a key resource to support the Army Reserve in Halifax. Phase one will reconstruct of the west wall. Phase two will rehabilitate the remaining walls and fit-up the armoury’s interior.

projects.title: Relocation of CF School of Aerospace Control Operations (CFSACO)
projects.objective: The objective of this project is to establish operationally effective and suitable infrastructure that supports the school’s specific training requirements. This includes eliminating leasing costs, consolidating student operations within a Wing to enhance professional development, improving access to administrative support, increasing exposure to pilots and tower/terminal operations, and potentially providing access to high-security facilities. Also, part of the objective is to accommodate all operational and support functions necessary to meet the school’s training objectives and expectations.

projects.title: Replace Refueling Tender Garage - 14 Wing Greenwood
projects.objective: Replace the existing refuelling tender facility of Greenwood.

projects.title: Replace underground electrical distribution system
projects.objective: This project will enhance the electrical distribution system on the Saint-Jean Garrison. The project will provide affordable and sustainable electrical distribution infrastructure to correct the degradation of the existing installations and support the imminent construction of many new buildings.
